title: Zhipu AI Shares Surge 35% in Hong Kong Despite Widening 2025 Losses
detail: Shares of Chinese AI startup Zhipu AI jumped up to 35% in Hong Kong trading on March 31 despite the company reporting a ~60% increase in 2025 net losses. Investors remained bullish on the company's agentic AI strategy and enterprise cloud platform. CEO Zhang Peng cited Anthropic as a benchmark for commercialization and forecast 'exponential growth' for its cloud model business. Zhipu is valued at approximately $14 billion. The market reaction highlights a broader bifurcation in China's AI investment landscape: while established tech companies like Alibaba and Tencent saw compressed valuations during the 2021 regulatory crackdown, dedicated AI-native startups like Zhipu, Moonshot AI, and MiniMax continue to attract premium multiples despite losses, mirroring the early OpenAI-era valuation dynamics in the US market.

title: NYC Mayor Mamdani Reverses City Government TikTok Ban
detail: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani announced the reversal of the 2023 city government ban on TikTok, allowing city agencies and public employees to use TikTok on specially designated government-issued devices that cannot access sensitive data, internal email systems, or restricted networks. The reversal follows the finalization of TikTok's US divestiture deal on January 22, 2026, under which Oracle, Silver Lake, and MGX now hold majority control of TikTok's US operations and US user data is stored exclusively on Oracle-managed servers. Mayor Mamdani framed the reversal as enabling New York City government agencies to communicate with constituents on a platform used by 170 million Americans. Critics including the Washington Examiner argued the reversal was premature given ByteDance's retained 19.9% stake and ongoing legal questions about the deal's national security protections.

title: ByteDance Plans $14B Nvidia GPU Purchase in 2026, Betting on US Export Control Easing
detail: ByteDance is reportedly planning to spend approximately 100 billion yuan (~$14 billion USD) on Nvidia H200 AI GPUs in 2026, according to Tom's Hardware citing industry sources. The plan represents a sharp increase from the 85 billion yuan ByteDance spent on Nvidia chips in 2025, and reflects the company's bet that the Trump administration will continue to allow advanced AI chip exports to China, or grant ByteDance-specific approvals given the TikTok ownership restructuring. ByteDance's dual strategy — pursuing domestic Huawei Ascend 950PR orders while simultaneously stockpiling Nvidia H200s — illustrates the 'hedge both ways' approach Chinese tech giants are adopting: building Huawei-compatible infrastructure while maintaining Nvidia H200 clusters for maximum AI training performance. The purchases, if approved, would partially offset Nvidia's revenue losses from the April 2025 H20 restriction.
